title: pittosporum revolutum
intro: Pittosporum revolutum, also known as the New Zealand tobacco tree or Kohuhu, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and is part of the Pittosporum genus. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to the presence of volatile oils and alkaloids. The health benefits of Pittosporum revolutum include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
